beats.narration: Everything converges on one skill: take an unfamiliar part and write a plan another tech can run, and an auditor can defend.

payload.title: PLAN THE SHOTS
payload.sub: Take any part. Write a plan you can defend.

beats.narration: We build three plans: a shaft on the wet bench, a weldment with prods in the yard, and a ring gear, no contact allowed.
Two directions for the shaft: the head shot's circular field catches axial seams; the coil's longitudinal field catches transverse cracks at journals and fillets.
A finished gear gets no electrical contact: an offset central conductor covers about four conductor diameters per shot, so rotate through overlapping shots.

payload.heading: Circular field, no electrical contact
payload.note: EACH SHOT COVERS ≈ 4 × CONDUCTOR Ø · OVERLAP

beats.narration: Three parts, one habit: plan it, prove it, defend it. A technique is just a plan that survives the question why.

payload.sub: A technique is a plan that survives the question why.
